name: Jetan (Martian Chess)
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs (1922) / AGP port

chief_rule.label: Chief captured by a lesser piece

description: The Martian chess from Edgar Rice Burroughs' The Chessmen of Mars (1922) - the only game of fiction whose complete rules the author published. Black (south) and Orange (north) armies of 20 fight on a 10x10 board; every piece moves an EXACT number of single-square steps and may turn at each step, Thoats, Fliers and the Princess leap over anything. Win by landing any piece on the enemy Princess, or Chief takes Chief; a Chief slain by a lesser piece ends the game in a draw (Burroughs) or is simply lost (option). The Princess cannot capture, may never end a move on a threatened square, and holds a one-time escape to any safe square. Ambiguities resolved per Abstract Games magazine's suggested standard.
